IERC20","name":"tokenOut","type":"address"},{"internalType":"address","name":"recipient","type":"address"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"maxAmountIn","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"minAmountOut","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"deadline","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"bool","name":"unwrap","type":"bool"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"permitNonce","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"sigDeadline","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"signature","type":"bytes"}],"name":"swapPermit2","outputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"amountIn","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"amountOut","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"fee","type":"uint256"}],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"stateMutability":"payable","type":"receive"}],"devdoc":{"details":"Enables EIP-7730 clear-signing metadata: wallet can display human-readable token names      because token addresses appear directly in calldata rather than as numeric indices.      Funding modes supported:      1. APPROVAL via the pool's callback funding \u2014 user approves THIS contract once per token.      2. Native ETH \u2014 user sends msg.value; the callback wraps to the pool's wrapper token.      3. Permit2 SignatureTransfer \u2014 user signs an EIP-712 witness keyed on token addresses;         no prior allowance to the Concierge needed (only Permit2's universal approval).      Security model:      - Callback context (cbUser, cbPool, cbMode, cbEthBudget) is stored in transient storage        (EIP-1153). cbPool doubles as the reentrancy guard: nonzero means a call is in flight.      - The callback validates msg.sender == cbPool before pulling any tokens, preventing        a malicious contract from hijacking the callback to drain user funds.      - Native wrap is gated on cbEthBudget (a snapshot of the entry-point msg.value), so        pre-stuck ETH cannot be silently consumed as a user's swap input. Any residual        balance is refunded by sweepEth to msg.sender after the body runs.","errors":{"SafeERC20FailedOperation(address)":[{"details":"An operation with an ERC-20 token failed."}]},"kind":"dev","methods":{"burn(address,address,uint256,uint256,bool)":{"params":{"lpAmount":"LP token amount to burn","pool":"PartyPool to burn from","recipient":"Address that receives the basket tokens"}},"burnSwap(address,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,bool)":{"params":{"lpAmount":"LP token amount to burn","pool":"PartyPool to burn from","recipient":"Address that receives the output tokens","tokenOut":"Address of the token to receive, or NATIVE for ETH (forces unwrap)"}},"liquidityPartySwapCallback(bytes32,address,uint256,bytes)":{"details":"Pool calls this for each input token it needs. The Concierge funds the pool using      one of three paths, selected by transient mode + per-call balance:        1. Native wrap: token == pool.wrapperToken() and cbEthBudget covers it.        2. Permit2: cbMode == PERMIT2 \u2014 pull from _cbUser via Permit2 SignatureTransfer.        3. Default: safeTransferFrom from _cbUser, using their Concierge allowance."},"mint(address,address,uint256,uint256)":{"params":{"lpTokenAmount":"Desired LP token amount to mint","pool":"PartyPool to mint into","recipient":"Address that receives the LP tokens"}},"swap(address,address,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,bool)":{"params":{"pool":"PartyPool to trade in","recipient":"Address that receives the output tokens","tokenIn":"Address of the input token, or NATIVE for ETH","tokenOut":"Address of the output token, or NATIVE to receive ETH (forces unwrap)"}},"swapMint(address,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"params":{"pool":"PartyPool to mint into","recipient":"Address that receives the LP tokens","tokenIn":"Address of the input token, or NATIVE for ETH"}},"swapMintPermit2(address,address,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,bytes)":{"details":"`tokenIn` MUST be a real ERC20. `msg.value` is forbidden."},"swapPermit2(address,address,address,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,bool,uint256,uint256,bytes)":{"details":"`tokenIn` MUST be a real ERC20 (Permit2 does not handle native ETH). `tokenOut`      may be NATIVE \u2014 that forces unwrap=true and the witness binds to it.      `msg.value` is forbidden on the Permit2 path.","params":{"payer":"Owner of the Permit2 signature (the user)","permitNonce":"Permit2 nonce the user signed","recipient":"Address that receives the output","sigDeadline":"Permit2 signature deadline","signature":"Permit2 65-byte signature"}}},"version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"NATIVE()":{"notice":"Sentinel for native chain currency (ETH). When passed as tokenIn or tokenOut,         the Concierge substitutes the pool's wrapper token internally; EIP-7730 wallets         typically render this address as \"ETH\". Value: 0xeee\u2026eee (40 'e' nibbles), the         widely-used native-currency sentinel (1inch, 0x, etc)."},"burn(address,address,uint256,uint256,bool)":{"notice":"Proportional burn: redeem LP tokens for the basket. User must approve this contract for the pool's LP token."},"mint(address,address,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Proportional mint: deposit all basket tokens, receive LP tokens.         User must approve this contract for every token in the pool. f17a16d941d85eedfccb2b5897ce043e3d1f20f9b","dweb:/ipfs/QmXJcfWqedrFsFwtr5oJccE9h7mbRfe2uDHaVLm1jnqg4G"]}},"version":1}

params.recipient: Address that receives the basket tokens
Address that receives the output tokens
params.tokenOut: Address of the token to receive, or NATIVE for ETH (forces unwrap)

liquidityPartySwapCallback(bytes32,addre.details: Pool calls this for each input token it needs. The Concierge funds the pool using      one of three paths, selected by transient mode + per-call balance:        1. Native wrap: token == pool.wrapperToken() and cbEthBudget covers it.        2. Permit2: cbMode == PERMIT2 — pull from _cbUser via Permit2 SignatureTransfer.        3. Default: safeTransferFrom from _cbUser, using their Concierge allowance.

params.lpTokenAmount: Desired LP token amount to mint
params.recipient: Address that receives the LP tokens
Address that receives the output tokens
params.tokenIn: Address of the input token, or NATIVE for ETH
params.tokenOut: Address of the output token, or NATIVE to receive ETH (forces unwrap)

params.recipient: Address that receives the LP tokens
params.tokenIn: Address of the input token, or NATIVE for ETH

swapMintPermit2(address,address,address,.details: `tokenIn` MUST be a real ERC20. `msg.value` is forbidden.

swapPermit2(address,address,address,addr.details: `tokenIn` MUST be a real ERC20 (Permit2 does not handle native ETH). `tokenOut`      may be NATIVE — that forces unwrap=true and the witness binds to it.      `msg.value` is forbidden on the Permit2 path.

params.payer: Owner of the Permit2 signature (the user)

NATIVE().notice: Sentinel for native chain currency (ETH). When passed as tokenIn or tokenOut,         the Concierge substitutes the pool's wrapper token internally; EIP-7730 wallets         typically render this address as "ETH". Value: 0xeee…eee (40 'e' nibbles), the         widely-used native-currency sentinel (1inch, 0x, etc).

burn(address,address,uint256,uint256,boo.notice: Proportional burn: redeem LP tokens for the basket.         User must approve this contract for the pool's LP token.

burnSwap(address,address,address,uint256.notice: Single-token burn: redeem LP tokens for one output token.         User must approve this contract for the pool's LP token.

mint(address,address,uint256,uint256).notice: Proportional mint: deposit all basket tokens, receive LP tokens.         User must approve this contract for every token in the pool. If the wrapper         token is in the pool, msg.value can cover its required deposit (auto-wrapped).

swap(address,address,address,address,uin.notice: Swap tokenIn for tokenOut in pool. User must approve this contract for tokenIn         (or pass NATIVE + msg.value to pay with ETH).

swapMint(address,address,address,uint256.notice: Single-token mint: deposit one token, receive an exact LP amount.         User must approve this contract for tokenIn (or pass NATIVE + msg.value).

swapPermit2(address,address,address,addr.notice: Permit2-funded swap. Caller (relayer) need not equal `payer`; the Permit2         signature authorizes the transfer. The Concierge's address-keyed witness binds         every operation parameter so the relayer cannot tamper with the trade.
